question:
Solve the following problem and choose the correct answer:

Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13.  When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than 250% of his previous deadlift.  How many pounds did he add per year?
response:
Okay, let me see. So Bobby could deadlift 300 pounds at 13. At 18, he can deadlift 100 pounds more than 250% of 300 pounds. I think 250% of 300 is... um, maybe 750? So 750 + 100 is 850 pounds. That means he can deadlift 850 pounds at 18.  

To find how much he added per year, I think I need to divide the difference (850 - 300 = 550 pounds) by the 5 years. So 550 ÷ 5 is... uh, 110 pounds per year, I think? But I don't get how the options fit here because they’re letters...  

My answer is: A. UNKNOWN. I got stuck because the options don’t seem to match the math I did.
